message:
refactor(vdbe): bundle agg-step helper args into a struct

`agg_step_with_args` was added in the previous commit with eight
positional arguments (cold_state, statement_cold_state, accum_reg,
is_distinct, func, func_name, agg_collation, execution_cx, args).
That tripped clippy's `too_many_arguments` lint and made the two
call sites' formatting awkward — a vertical column of bare values
that's easy to mis-align when adding a parameter later.

Bundles the seven "describe this step" parameters into a new
private struct:

    struct AggStepCall<'a> {
        accum_reg: i32,
        is_distinct: bool,
        func: &'a Arc<ErasedAggregateFunction>,
        func_name: &'a str,
        agg_collation: Option<&'a str>,
        execution_cx: &'a Cx,
        args: &'a [SqliteValue],
    }

The two state-mutation parameters (`cold_state` and
`statement_cold_state`) stay as direct `&mut` arguments — they
mutate, and threading them through the struct would require
self-referential borrows or moving them into the struct, both of
which are worse than the current shape.

Helper signature becomes:

    fn agg_step_with_args(
        cold_state: &mut Option<Box<ColdVdbeState>>,
        statement_cold_state: &mut StatementColdState,
        step: AggStepCall<'_>,
    ) -> Result<()>

Both call sites (the in-place fast path and the
collect_reg_range fallback) now build an `AggStepCall { ... }` in
named-field form, which is easier to scan and stops misalignment
as parameters change. Struct construction is zero-cost (all
fields are references and small Copy types) so this is purely a
readability change.

Inside the helper, every `args` / `func` / `func_name` / etc.
reference becomes `step.args` / `step.func` / etc. No semantic
changes; the body of the helper is identical modulo field-access
prefixes.

`#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]` was *not* added because
the lint trigger is now legitimately gone.

No public API change; struct is private to the module.
